sales-tax.customIntro: Iowa has a 6% state sales tax rate, and most cities and counties have adopted an additional 1% local option sales tax (LOST), making the effective combined rate 7% across most of the state. Iowa updated its local option tax landscape in January 2026, with several jurisdictions removing sunset clauses to make their local taxes permanent.
sales-tax.formula: Price × 0.07 = sales tax owed (most Iowa counties example)

faqs.question: What is the sales tax rate in Iowa?
faqs.answer: Iowa's state sales tax rate is 6%. Most Iowa cities and counties have adopted a 1% local option sales tax (LOST), bringing the effective combined rate to 7% across the majority of the state. A small number of rural areas without a local option tax pay only the 6% state rate.

faqs.question: What is the sales tax rate in Des Moines, Iowa?
faqs.answer: Des Moines is in Polk County, which has a combined sales tax rate of 7% — the 6% state rate plus a 1% local option sales tax. This is the standard rate for most major Iowa cities including Cedar Rapids, Davenport, Iowa City, and Waterloo.

faqs.question: Does Iowa have a sales tax holiday?
faqs.answer: No. Iowa does not have a general sales tax holiday or tax-free weekend.

faqs.question: What is exempt from sales tax in Iowa?
faqs.answer: Iowa exempts most unprepared groceries and prescription drugs from sales tax. Farm machinery and equipment used directly in agricultural production is also exempt. Most clothing is taxable in Iowa — unlike neighboring Minnesota and Illinois where clothing has different treatment.

faqs.question: Is clothing taxable in Iowa?
faqs.answer: Yes. Most clothing and footwear is fully taxable in Iowa at the standard combined rate. Iowa does not have a clothing exemption, unlike neighboring states Minnesota (which permanently exempts clothing) or Illinois.

faqs.question: Do you pay sales tax on a used car in Iowa?
faqs.answer: Iowa charges a one-time registration fee instead of traditional sales tax on vehicle purchases. This fee is 5% of the purchase price for most vehicles and is paid at the time of registration at the county treasurer's office. It replaces the standard 6%–7% sales tax rate that would otherwise apply.
